evals.prompt: We are preparing for our year-end audit. Can you review our Procure-to-Pay process for potential gaps? We deal with about 50 vendors.
evals.expected_output: Should identify key audit objectives for P2P: Existence, Completeness, and Valuation. Should focus on the 'Three-Way Match' control. Should suggest a search for unrecorded liabilities by looking at post-year-end payments. Should provide a structured list of findings with Risk ratings and Recommendations.
assertions: Identifies cut-off testing as a priority
Output follows Audit Findings Report structure
Each finding has Issue, Risk, and Recommendation

evals.prompt: Plan the audit of revenue for a company where 70% of sales run through a December channel-stuffing-prone distributor arrangement.
evals.expected_output: Elevated risk assessment on cut-off and returns: side-letter inquiry, post-period credit note review, distributor confirmation of sell-through vs sell-in terms, analytics on December vs January patterns.
assertions: Identifies cut-off and right-of-return as the key assertions at risk
Tests post-period returns and credit notes
Considers side agreements and confirms terms with the distributor
Uses month-over-month analytics targeting period-end spikes

evals.prompt: Management refuses to provide the legal confirmation letter for a pending lawsuit the lawyers say is 'probable and material'. What are my options as auditor?
evals.expected_output: Scope limitation on a material item: escalate to those charged with governance, evaluate alternative procedures, and if unresolved consider qualified opinion or disclaimer; also reassess management integrity and fraud risk.
assertions: Treats it as a scope limitation, not an inconvenience
Escalates to governance before opinion modification
Maps the outcome to qualified/disclaimer options

evals.prompt: Review the attached control register (evals/files/control_register.csv) as part of interim audit planning. Identify control gaps, segregation-of-duties conflicts, and overdue performance; classify severity and propose the audit response for each.
evals.expected_output: Findings: C-02 missing evidence for two months (operating effectiveness cannot be relied on - extend substantive testing on credit notes); C-03 preparer-approver SoD conflict on bank recs (design deficiency); C-05 user access review two quarters overdue (ITGC gap - affects reliance on automated controls incl. C-01). C-01 and C-04 satisfactory.
assertions: Flags C-02's missing April-May evidence and its impact on control reliance
Identifies the C-03 segregation-of-duties conflict as a design deficiency
Flags C-05 as two quarters overdue and links the ITGC gap to reliance on automated controls
Does not raise findings on C-01 or C-04
Proposes a concrete audit response per finding
